body:
## Summary

- Addresses #96 by hardening Rust Google Docs browser-model capture: Chrome gets a bounded diagnostic attempt, timed-out Chrome children are killed on drop, and the model path falls back to editor HTML when `--dump-dom` does not return.
- Avoids the Windows hosted-runner Google Docs browser-model hang by using the editor HTML model fallback directly on Windows and bounding that HTTP fallback.
- Fixes model markdown regressions in JS and Rust: duplicate empty table columns, same-list joining, multi-paragraph blockquote continuation, nested bold/italic marker balancing, and final trailing newline.
- Improves Google Docs public-export preprocessing in both runtimes by hoisting generated CSS class styles into semantic tags, converting CSS-indented paragraphs into blockquotes, stripping standalone empty anchors, and preserving the existing redirect/NBSP cleanup.
- Archives the issue data, before/after logs, reference captures, failed CI log, and case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-96/`.

## Verification

- `cd js && npm run check`
- `cd js && npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/gdocs.test.js`
- `cd js && npm test -- --testPathIgnorePatterns="docker.test.js"`
- `cd js && GDOCS_INTEGRATION=true npm test -- --testPathPattern="gdocs-public-doc" --testTimeout=120000`
- `cd rust && cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cd rust && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cd rust && cargo test --test integration gdocs -- --nocapture`
- `cd rust && cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cd rust && cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cd rust && GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 cargo test --test integration gdocs_public_doc::live -- --nocapture`
- `cd rust && GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 cargo test --test integration gdocs_public_doc::live_browser_model_capture_of_public_document_preserves_markdown_features -- --nocapture`
- `cd rust && cargo build --release`
- `cd rust && cargo package --list --allow-dirty`

Docker e2e was not run locally. The non-Docker e2e/process tests and Google Docs live checks passed locally.

## CI Follow-up

The first current-head CI run exposed a Windows-only timeout in `Rust - Test (windows-latest)` while running the Google Docs browser-model live test. The downloaded log is archived at `docs/case-studies/issue-96/ci-logs/rust-checks-24714236603.log`; lines 4879-4881 show the export live test passed, then the browser-model live test ran for over 60 seconds and was killed by the 10-minute step timeout.

## Case Study

The case study is in `docs/case-studies/issue-96/README.md`. It preserves the raw issue/PR/package data, before/after regression logs, live smoke logs, public export HTML, CI timeout log, and captured outputs including `reference/rust-browser-gdocs-after.md`.

Known follow-up: public-export nested list structure, converter-specific H1/H2 heading style, and adjacent same-target styled-link merging are still generic converter limitations. They are documented in the case study separately from the fixed browser/model regressions.


Fixes #96
title: Fix Google Docs capture regressions for issue 96

body:
## Summary

Closes #90.

- Implements real Google Docs `--capture browser` model capture for both CLIs against the public reference document: JS intercepts `DOCS_modelChunk` assignments and pushed chunks in a real browser, while Rust now launches Chrome/Chromium headless and parses the real browser DOM instead of fetching `/edit` over HTTP.
- Extends the Google Docs model parsers to consume live `ty: "as"` style records for headings, inline bold/italic/strikethrough, links, lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, image alt text, one-based image anchors, and Rust UTF-16 position mapping.
- Adds live JS and Rust public-document browser tests that verify the real document renders key Markdown features from `DOCS_modelChunk`, plus parser regression tests for pushed model items and UTF-16 positions.
- Preserves the issue #90 case study, reference fixture data, experiment script, and selected validation/model logs under `docs/case-studies/issue-90/`.

## Scope Notes

- `--capture browser` is now the implemented path for the issue #90 real-browser requirement; it does not silently fall back to public export.
- `--capture api` live tests still guard public-export content/section preservation. Full Drive export HTML formatting parity remains a separate limitation because the export HTML uses inline CSS and escaped heading prefixes.

## Validation

- [x] `npm test -- --testPathPattern="gdocs" --runInBand`
- [x] `GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 npm test -- --testPathPattern="gdocs-public-doc" --runInBand --testTimeout=120000`
- [x] `npm run lint`
- [x] `npm run format:check`
- [x] `npm test -- --testPathIgnorePatterns="docker.test.js"`
- [x] `cargo test --test integration gdocs:: -- --nocapture`
- [x] `GDOCS_INTEGRATION=1 cargo test --test integration gdocs_public_doc::live -- --nocapture`
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- [x] `cargo test --all-features --verbose`

Validation logs are committed under `docs/case-studies/issue-90/logs/`.
title: Implement real Google Docs browser model capture

body:
## Summary

Implements Google Docs document capture support, addressing all requirements from issue #36:

- **Google Docs capture module** (`gdocs.js` / `gdocs.rs`): Detects Google Docs URLs, extracts document IDs, and fetches documents via the Google Docs export URL pattern (`/export?format=html|md|txt|pdf|docx|epub|zip`)
- **API token support**: `--apiToken` CLI option and `API_TOKEN` environment variable for private document access. API server supports `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and `X-Api-Token` headers
- **`/gdocs` API endpoint**: New endpoint in both Express.js and Axum servers
- **Auto-detection**: CLI automatically detects Google Docs URLs and uses API-based capture
- **Configurable capture**: `--capture` option to choose between `browser` and `api` capture methods
- **Archive mode**: `format=archive` extracts embedded base64 images from Google Docs HTML exports and bundles them with markdown and HTML into a ZIP archive (`article.md`, `article.html`, `images/`)
- **HTML entity decoding**: All HTML and Markdown extraction pipelines decode HTML entities to unicode characters (e.g., `&` → `&`, `—` → `—`) using `html-escape` (Rust) and `he` (JS)
- **Unicode by default**: All export methods request data in UTF-8 and convert to unicode
- **Rust tests reorganized**: All tests moved from inline `#[cfg(test)]` modules in `src/` to separate test files in `tests/` folder for better visibility and organization
- **lino-arguments updated** from `^0.2.1` to `^0.2.5` (latest)
- **JS and Rust in sync**: Both implementations have identical functionality

## Changes

### Google Docs Module
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `js/src/gdocs.js` | Google Docs capture: URL detection, export URL construction, fetch, archive mode, image extraction |
| `rust/src/gdocs.rs` | Rust Google Docs capture module (matching JS) with archive and image extraction |
| `js/src/index.js` | Register `/gdocs` endpoint |
| `rust/src/main.rs` | Add `/gdocs` endpoint with archive/zip format support |

### HTML Entity Decoding
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `js/src/lib.js` | Add `he.decode()` to HTML-to-Markdown conversion pipelines |
| `js/src/gdocs.js` | Decode HTML entities for text-based export formats |
| `rust/src/html.rs` | Add `decode_html_entities()` and `normalize_url()` public functions |
| `rust/src/markdown.rs` | Decode HTML entities after html2md conversion |
| `rust/src/gdocs.rs` | Decode entities for text-based formats, unicode request headers |
| `rust/Cargo.toml` | Add `html-escape` and `zip` dependencies |
| `js/package.json` | Add `he` dependency |

### Test Reorganization
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `rust/tests/*_tests.rs` | 14 test files moved from inline modules (110+ tests total) |
| `js/tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` | 27 unit tests including image extraction tests |

## Test plan

- [x] Rust: 110 tests pass across 14 test files (0 failures)
- [x] Rust: clippy clean (pedantic + nursery lints)
- [x] JS: 188 unit tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] JS: 16 browser tests skipped (pre-existing, require Chromium)
- [x] HTML entity decoding tested for basic entities, unicode, and named entities
- [x] Base64 image extraction tested for single/multiple/no images and mixed content
- [ ] Manual test: `web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format markdown`
- [ ] Manual test: API `/gdocs?url=...&format=archive` returns ZIP with images

### Usage examples

```bash
# CLI: Capture public Google Doc as Markdown
web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format markdown

# CLI: Capture as archive (ZIP with markdown + HTML + images)
web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format archive

# API: Get Markdown
GET /gdocs?url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit&format=markdown

# API: Get archive with images
GET /gdocs?url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit&format=archive

# API: With auth for private documents
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" "http://localhost:3000/gdocs?url=...&format=archive"
```

Fixes #36

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add Google Docs (document to markdown) support

body:
## Summary

Fixes #72.

Adds explicit Google Docs capture-method routing for both CLIs:

- `--capture browser` now uses the editor `/edit` model path instead of the public export endpoint.
- `--capture api` without a token keeps the existing public `/export?format=...` behavior.
- `--capture api` with an API token uses `https://docs.googleapis.com/v1/documents/<DOC_ID>`.
- JS browser capture installs an init script to intercept `DOCS_modelChunk`, extracts `docs-images-rt` CID mappings, and renders Markdown/HTML/text.
- Rust now exposes matching capture selection, Docs API rendering, and model parsing helpers; its browser-model path parses embedded `/edit` model chunks where available.
- Added issue #72 case-study artifacts under `docs/case-studies/issue-72/`.
- Added gated verbose diagnostics for Google Docs capture. JavaScript uses `log-lazy` behind `--verbose`; Rust uses `tracing` debug output behind `--verbose` or `RUST_LOG`.

This branch also includes current `main` with PR #74, so the removed `/gdocs` HTTP route is not reintroduced.

## Reproduction

Before the fix, Google Docs URL detection ran before the normal browser path, so `--capture browser` still entered the export-based `fetchGoogleDoc*` / `fetch_google_doc*` path. The new regression tests initially failed because the capture-selection/model/API functions did not exist.

The latest review request also required detailed verbose diagnostics. The new logging records method selection, endpoint URLs, response status/content type, body sizes, model chunk counts, CID mapping counts, rendered output sizes, and parsed block/table/image counts without changing default output.

## Tests

- `npm install`
- `npm test -- --runInBand tests/unit/gdocs.test.js`
- `npm test -- --runInBand --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/unit/browser.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/integration/browser-engines.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/integration/habr-article.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/e2e/docker.test.js`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run format:check`
- `npm run check:duplication`
- `node --check js/bin/web-capture.js`
- `node --check js/src/gdocs.js`
- `node ../scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`
- `cargo test`
- `cargo test --test integration gdocs -- --nocapture`
- `cargo fmt -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `git diff --check`

Full JS Jest was also attempted. It reached browser-dependent suites and failed locally because Playwright browser binaries were not installed in that environment; the log is preserved at `docs/case-studies/issue-72/js-full-test-browser-missing.log`.

## Case Study

See `docs/case-studies/issue-72/README.md` for timeline, preserved issue/PR data, root cause analysis, solution options, external references, and validation logs.
title: feat: support Google Docs capture methods

body:
## Summary

Fixes #81.

Adds a safety-net fallback for `--capture browser` on Google Docs: when the editor page does not expose `DOCS_modelChunk` data, the JS CLI now warns and falls back to the Google Docs public export pipeline instead of failing or returning a lossy capture. Archive fallback reuses `fetchGoogleDocAsArchive`, so embedded base64 images are still extracted into the archive.

This complements the browser-model improvements from #93 (issue #92 R1–R6): when the model is available (the typical case) we still use it with all the formatting fixes; this PR only kicks in as a lossless fallback when the model is unreachable.

## Changes

- `js/src/gdocs-fallback.js` (new) — houses `captureGoogleDocWithBrowserOrFallback`, `fetchGoogleDocByExportFormat`, `isGoogleDocsBrowserModelUnavailableError`, and `googleDocsBrowserModelUnavailableError`. Kept in its own module so `js/src/gdocs.js` stays under the 1500-line lint limit.
- `js/src/gdocs.js` — throws the sentinel `googleDocsBrowserModelUnavailableError` when `blocks.length === 0`, re-exports the fallback helpers, and exposes thin wrappers that inject sibling fetchers.
- `js/bin/web-capture.js` — calls `captureGoogleDocWithBrowserOrFallback`; archive mode localizes model images when the browser-model path succeeds, and uses the export archive result directly when fallback kicks in.
- `js/tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` — adds `captureGoogleDocWithBrowserOrFallback (issue #81)`, simulating zero model chunks and verifying archive fallback preserves an embedded image.
- `js/.changeset/gdocs-browser-export-fallback.md` — patch changeset.

## Reproduction

Before this change, a Google Docs `/edit` page that exposes no `DOCS_modelChunk` data caused the browser-model path to throw `Google Docs editor page did not expose DOCS_modelChunk data`, leaving browser capture unable to preserve content or images. The new unit test reproduces that state with a fake browser returning zero model chunks and a mocked Google Docs HTML export response with an embedded image.

## Verification

- `cd js && npm run lint` — 0 errors
- `cd js && npm run format:check` — clean
- `cd js && npm run check:duplication` — clean
- `cd js && npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` — 55/55 pass (includes the new fallback test and the merged R5/R6 tests from #93)
- `cd js && npm test -- --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/e2e/docker.test.js` — 295/295 pass
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passed

Docker E2E not run locally (no `docker` binary); CI runs it.
title: fix(js): fall back for Google Docs browser capture
